reasoning: There is currently no vaccine for Trachoma. A recent phase 1 clinical trial for a candidate vaccine (chlamydia vaccine CTH522) was published in April 2024. Since phase 1 trials evaluate early safety and immunogenicity, the vaccine is still years away from phase 3 efficacy trials and regulatory approval. It is extraordinarily unlikely that a Trachoma vaccine will be developed and officially recognized on Wikipedia by any of the resolution dates falling between November 2025 and February 2026. The probabilities have been set very low, increasing slightly over time to account for a marginally longer horizon, although the chance remains minuscule.
